description: Create the ClassSelectionScreen UI, generate class-specific player sprites, integrate class selection into the game flow (IntroScreen -> ClassSelectionScreen -> GameScreen), update MainMenuScreen to show class on Continue, and update MetaProgression to track per-class stats.
steps: Create src/view/screens/ClassSelectionScreen.java - A new Screen implementation following the pattern of SkillChoiceScreen and IntroScreen. Shows 3 class options with pixel art sprites, class name, description, stat preview (HP/ATK/DEF/MP), starting skills list. Navigate with 1/2/3 keys or arrow keys + Enter, or mouse click. Confirm selection before starting. Color identity: Knight=Steel blue/silver, Mage=Purple/gold, Rogue=Dark green/crimson. After selection, create CombatEngine, call startGame(playerName, selectedClass), apply unlocks, and transition to GameScreen.

Add to ColorPalette.java - Add class-specific colors: KNIGHT_BLUE (steel blue ~#4A7FB5), KNIGHT_SILVER (~#C8D0D8), MAGE_PURPLE (~#7B2FBE), MAGE_GOLD (~#FFD700 - can reuse HOLY_GOLD), ROGUE_GREEN (~#2D5A27), ROGUE_CRIMSON (~#8B1A1A).

Modify src/view/sprites/SpriteGenerator.java - Add methods generateKnightSprite(), generateMageSprite(), generateRogueSprite() that create class-specific player sprites (24x32 like existing player). Knight: heavy plate armor with shield and longsword, steel blue tint. Mage: flowing robes with staff, purple/gold colors. Rogue: hooded cloak with twin daggers, dark green/crimson. Register them as 'player_knight', 'player_mage', 'player_rogue' in entitySprites. Call these from generateAll().

Modify src/view/screens/IntroScreen.java - After name entry is confirmed and story is complete (READY_TO_START state), instead of calling startGame() which creates CombatEngine directly, transition to new ClassSelectionScreen passing the player name. Change startGame() to accept a transition to ClassSelectionScreen(game, playerName.toString()).

Modify src/view/screens/MainMenuScreen.java - In renderMainMenu(), when showing Continue option and a save exists, load SaveData to get characterClass field and display it next to 'Continue' (e.g., 'Continue (Knight - Wave 5)'). Show the class name in the menu.

Modify src/controller/MetaProgression.java - Add per-class tracking fields: knightVictories, mageVictories, rogueVictories (ints). Update recordRunEnd to accept characterClass parameter. Update toJson/fromJson to include new fields. Add getters for class-specific stats.

Modify src/view/screens/GameScreen.java - When determining which player sprite to use, check the player's CharacterClass and use the corresponding sprite key ('player_knight', 'player_mage', 'player_rogue') instead of always 'player'. If characterClass is null (legacy), fall back to 'player'.
Modify src/view/screens/CombatEngineRestorer.java - When loading a saved game with a class, ensure the correct sprite key is used by passing class to engine.

Ensure the ClassSelectionScreen shows stat previews formatted nicely: HP, ATK, DEF, MP as bar-like indicators or numbers, plus list of 4 starting skills with element colors, plus class passive description.
acceptance_criteria: Running ./gradlew classes compiles with no errors
ClassSelectionScreen.java exists and implements Screen with all 3 class options
SpriteGenerator creates distinct sprites for knight, mage, and rogue
IntroScreen transitions to ClassSelectionScreen after name entry instead of directly to GameScreen
MainMenuScreen shows character class in Continue option when save exists
findings: All steps completed successfully. ClassSelectionScreen follows SkillChoiceScreen pattern with 3 panels. HUD sprite key mechanism uses a setter called from GameScreen constructor. MetaProgression recordRunEnd overloaded to accept CharacterClass for per-class tracking. IntroScreen simplified to just transition to ClassSelectionScreen. The class selection and game start logic moved entirely into ClassSelectionScreen.selectClass().
